analysis: The Hindi translation is of high quality, effectively balancing technical terminology with natural-sounding Hindi prose. It correctly preserves MDX components and code logic while translating the surrounding context. The use of 'संकेत' for 'Hint' and 'कुंजी' for 'key' shows good linguistic judgment for a technical audience.
strengths: Excellent preservation of MDX structure and component props.
Natural translation of technical explanations (e.g., explaining the Promise constructor behavior).
Consistent terminology for 'Handling Errors' (त्रुटि हैंडलिंग/प्रबंधन) and 'Handling Data' (डेटा हैंडलिंग).
Correct adjustment of relative image paths in the final challenge.

issues.note: Slight inconsistency in translating 'Handling Errors' across different challenges (त्रुटियाँ संभालना vs त्रुटि हैंडलिंग vs त्रुटि प्रबंधन).
The translation of 'skillz' as 'स्किल्स' loses the informal/slang nuance of the source, though it remains clear.
